children.name: Rhizoprionodon
children.description: Rhizoprionodon — a genus of requiem sharks in the family Carcharhinidae.

children.name: Rhizoprionodon acutus
children.description: The milk shark is a species of requiem shark, and part of the family Carcharhinidae, whose common name comes from an old Indian belief that consumption of its meat promotes lactation. The largest and most widely distributed member of its genus, the milk shark typically measures 1.1 m (3.6 ft) long, and can be found in coastal tropical waters throughout the eastern Atlantic and the Indo-Pacific regions. Occurring from the surface to a depth of 200 m (660 ft), this species is common near beaches and in estuaries, and has been recorded swimming up rivers in Cambodia.

children.name: Rhizoprionodon lalandii
children.description: The Brazilian sharpnose shark is a requiem shark of the family Carcharhinidae. It is found in the tropical waters of the western Atlantic Ocean between latitudes 13° N and 33° S, at depths between 3 and 70 m. It has been recorded in the following countries: Aruba, Brazil, Colombia, French Guiana, Guyana, Panama, Suriname, Trinidad and Tobago and Venezuela.

children.name: Rhizoprionodon longurio
children.description: The Pacific sharpnose shark often migrates along the Pacific coast of Mexico. They are most commonly caught in the artisanal fishery of Mazatlán. When a large sample of Pacific sharpnose sharks were observed scientists concluded that this type of shark was a "viviparous shark of small size which is born at an average length of 31 cm." Their gestation period is between ten and eleven months.

children.name: Rhizoprionodon oligolinx
children.description: The grey sharpnose shark is a requiem shark of the family Carcharhinidae. It is found in the tropical waters of the Indo-West Pacific Oceans, between latitudes 30° N and 18° S, from the surface to a depth of 36 m. It can reach a length of about 70 cm.

children.name: Rhizoprionodon porosus
children.description: The Caribbean sharpnose shark is a requiem shark, and part of the family Carcharhinidae.

children.name: Rhizoprionodon spec
children.description: Rhizoprionodon spec — a species in the genus Rhizoprionodon, family Carcharhinidae. Also known as requiem sharks.

children.name: Rhizoprionodon taxandriae
children.description: Rhizoprionodon taxandriae — a species in the genus Rhizoprionodon, family Carcharhinidae. Also known as requiem sharks.

children.name: Rhizoprionodon taylori
children.description: The Australian sharpnose shark is a requiem shark, belonging to the family Carcharhinidae. It is found in the tropical waters of the western Pacific Ocean off Papua New Guinea and northern Australia, between latitudes 8°N and 28°S, from the surface to a depth of 110 m. It can grow up to a length around 70 cm.

children.name: Rhizoprionodon terraenovae
children.description: The Atlantic sharpnose shark is a species of requiem shark in the family Carcharhinidae. It is found in the subtropical waters of the north-western Atlantic Ocean, between latitudes 43°N and 18°N.

children.name: Scoliodon ganntourensis
children.description: Scoliodon ganntourensis — a species in the genus Rhizoprionodon, family Carcharhinidae. Also known as requiem sharks.

children.name: Carcharhinus
children.description: Carcharhinus — a genus of requiem sharks in the family Carcharhinidae.

children.name: Carcharhinus amblyrhynchoides
children.description: Carcharhinus amblyrhynchoides — a species in the genus Carcharhinus, family Carcharhinidae. Also known as requiem sharks.

children.name: Carcharhinus amblyrhynchos
children.description: The grey reef shark or gray reef shark is a species of requiem shark, in the family Carcharhinidae. One of the most common reef sharks in the Indo-Pacific, it is found as far east as Easter Island and as far west as South Africa. This species is most often seen in shallow water near the drop-offs of coral reefs.

children.name: Carcharhinus amboinensis
children.description: Carcharhinus amboinensis — a species in the genus Carcharhinus, family Carcharhinidae. Also known as requiem sharks.

children.name: Carcharhinus balochenisis
children.description: Carcharhinus balochenisis — a species in the genus Carcharhinus, family Carcharhinidae. Also known as requiem sharks.

children.name: Carcharhinus borneensis
children.description: Carcharhinus borneensis — a species in the genus Carcharhinus, family Carcharhinidae. Also known as requiem sharks.

children.name: Carcharhinus brachyurus
children.description: The copper shark, bronze whaler, or narrowtooth shark is a species of requiem shark found mostly in temperate latitudes. It is distributed in a number of separate populations in the northeastern and southwestern Atlantic, off southern Africa, in the northwestern and eastern Pacific, and around Australia and New Zealand, with scattered reports from equatorial regions. The species can be found from brackish rivers and estuaries to shallow bays and harbors, to offshore waters 100 m (330 ft) deep or more.

children.name: Carcharhinus brevipinna
children.description: The spinner shark is a type of requiem shark, in the family Carcharhinidae, named for the spinning leaps it makes as a part of its feeding strategy. This species occurs in tropical and warm temperate waters worldwide, except for in the eastern Pacific Ocean. It is found from coastal to offshore habitats to a depth of 100 m (330 ft), though it prefers shallow water.

children.name: Carcharhinus caquetius
children.description: Carcharhinus caquetius — a species in the genus Carcharhinus, family Carcharhinidae. Also known as requiem sharks.

children.name: Carcharhinus cautus
children.description: Carcharhinus cautus — a species in the genus Carcharhinus, family Carcharhinidae. Also known as requiem sharks.

children.name: Carcharhinus cerdale
children.description: Carcharhinus cerdale — a species in the genus Carcharhinus, family Carcharhinidae. Also known as requiem sharks.

children.name: Carcharhinus coatesi
children.description: Carcharhinus coatesi — a species in the genus Carcharhinus, family Carcharhinidae. Also known as requiem sharks.

children.name: Carcharhinus dussumieri
children.description: Carcharhinus dussumieri — a species in the genus Carcharhinus, family Carcharhinidae. Also known as requiem sharks.

children.name: Carcharhinus egertoni
children.description: Carcharhinus egertoni — a species in the genus Carcharhinus, family Carcharhinidae. Also known as requiem sharks.

children.name: Carcharhinus falciformis
children.description: The silky shark, also known by numerous names such as blackspot shark, gray whaler shark, olive shark, ridgeback shark, sickle shark, sickle-shaped shark and sickle silk shark, is a species of requiem shark, in the family Carcharhinidae, named for the smooth texture of its skin. It is one of the most abundant sharks in the pelagic zone, and can be found around the world in tropical waters. Highly mobile and migratory, this shark is most often found over the edge of the continental shelf down to 50 m (164 ft).

children.name: Carcharhinus fitzroyensis
children.description: Carcharhinus fitzroyensis — a species in the genus Carcharhinus, family Carcharhinidae. Also known as requiem sharks.

children.name: Carcharhinus galapagensis

children.name: Carcharhinus gilmorei

children.name: Carcharhinus hemiodon

children.name: Carcharhinus humani
children.description: Carcharhinus humani — a species in the genus Carcharhinus, family Carcharhinidae. Also known as requiem sharks.

children.name: Carcharhinus isodon
children.description: Carcharhinus isodon — a species in the genus Carcharhinus, family Carcharhinidae. Also known as requiem sharks.

children.name: Carcharhinus kasserinensis
children.description: Carcharhinus kasserinensis — a species in the genus Carcharhinus, family Carcharhinidae. Also known as requiem sharks.

children.name: Carcharhinus leiodon
children.description: Carcharhinus leiodon — a species in the genus Carcharhinus, family Carcharhinidae. Also known as requiem sharks.

children.name: Carcharhinus leucas
children.description: The bull shark, also known as the Zambezi shark in Africa and Lake Nicaragua shark in Nicaragua, is a species of requiem shark commonly found worldwide in warm, shallow waters along coasts and in rivers. It is known for its aggressive nature, and presence mainly in warm, shallow brackish and freshwater systems including estuaries and (usually) lower reaches of rivers. Their aggressive nature has led to ongoing shark-culling efforts near beaches to protect beachgoers, which is one of the causes of bull shark populations continuing to decrease.

children.name: Carcharhinus limbatus
children.description: The blacktip shark is a species of requiem shark in the family Carcharhinidae. It is common to coastal tropical and subtropical waters around the world, including brackish habitats. Genetic analyses have revealed substantial variation within this species, with populations from the western Atlantic Ocean isolated and distinct from those in the rest of its range.

children.name: Carcharhinus longimanus
children.description: The oceanic whitetip shark is a large requiem shark inhabiting the pelagic zone of tropical and warm temperate seas. It has a stocky body and iconic elongated rounded fins, with white tips. The species is typically solitary, though they may gather in large numbers at food concentrations.

children.name: Carcharhinus macloti
children.description: Carcharhinus macloti — a species in the genus Carcharhinus, family Carcharhinidae. Also known as requiem sharks.

children.name: Carcharhinus macrops
children.description: Carcharhinus macrops — a species in the genus Carcharhinus, family Carcharhinidae. Also known as requiem sharks.

children.name: Carcharhinus magdalenae
children.description: Carcharhinus magdalenae — a species in the genus Carcharhinus, family Carcharhinidae. Also known as requiem sharks.

children.name: Carcharhinus mancinae
children.description: Carcharhinus mancinae — a species in the genus Carcharhinus, family Carcharhinidae. Also known as requiem sharks.

children.name: Carcharhinus melanopterus
children.description: The blacktip reef shark is a species of requiem shark, in the family Carcharhinidae, which can be easily identified by the prominent black tips on its fins. Among the most abundant sharks inhabiting the tropical coral reefs of the Indian and Pacific Oceans, this species prefers shallow, inshore waters. Its exposed first dorsal fin is a common sight in the region.

children.name: Carcharhinus nigeriensis
children.description: Carcharhinus nigeriensis — a species in the genus Carcharhinus, family Carcharhinidae. Also known as requiem sharks.

children.name: Carcharhinus obscurus
children.description: The dusky shark is a species of requiem shark, in the family Carcharhinidae, occurring in tropical and warm-temperate continental seas worldwide. A generalist apex predator, the dusky shark can be found from the coast to the outer continental shelf and adjacent pelagic waters, and has been recorded from a depth of 400 m (1,300 ft). Populations migrate seasonally towards the poles in the summer and towards the equator in the winter, traveling hundreds to thousands of kilometers.

children.name: Carcharhinus obsoletus
children.description: Carcharhinus obsoletus — a species in the genus Carcharhinus, family Carcharhinidae. Also known as requiem sharks.

children.name: Carcharhinus perezii
children.description: Carcharhinus perezii — a species in the genus Carcharhinus, family Carcharhinidae. Also known as requiem sharks.

children.name: Carcharhinus perseus

children.name: Carcharhinus plumbeus

children.name: Carcharhinus porosus
children.description: Carcharhinus porosus — a species in the genus Carcharhinus, family Carcharhinidae. Also known as requiem sharks.

children.name: Carcharhinus priscus
children.description: Carcharhinus priscus — a species in the genus Carcharhinus, family Carcharhinidae. Also known as requiem sharks.

children.name: Carcharhinus sealei
children.description: Carcharhinus sealei — a species in the genus Carcharhinus, family Carcharhinidae. Also known as requiem sharks.

children.name: Carcharhinus signatus
children.description: The night shark is a species of requiem shark, in the family Carcharhinidae, found in the temperate and tropical waters of the Atlantic Ocean. An inhabitant of the outer continental shelf and upper continental slope, this shark most commonly occurs at depths of 50–600 m (160–1,970 ft) and conducts a diel vertical migration, spending the day in deeper water and moving into shallower waters at night. Off northeastern Brazil, large numbers congregate around seamounts of varying depths.

children.name: Galeocerdo cuvier
children.description: The tiger shark is a species of ground shark, and the only extant member of the genus Galeocerdo and family Galeocerdonidae. It is a large predator, with females capable of attaining a length of over 5 m. Populations are found in many tropical and temperate waters, especially around central Pacific islands.

children.name: Glyphis
children.description: Glyphis — a genus of requiem sharks in the family Carcharhinidae.

children.name: Glyphis fowlerae
children.description: The Ganges shark is a critically endangered species of requiem shark found in the Ganges River and the Brahmaputra River of India and Bangladesh. Additionally, genetic evidence has shown that both the Borneo river shark and Irrawaddy river shark should be regarded as synonyms of the Ganges shark, expanding the range of the species to Pakistan, Myanmar, Borneo, and Java. The species remains poorly known and is very rare throughout its range.

children.name: Glyphis gangeticus
children.description: The Ganges shark is a critically endangered species of requiem shark found in the Ganges River and the Brahmaputra River of India and Bangladesh. Additionally, genetic evidence has shown that both the Borneo river shark and Irrawaddy river shark should be regarded as synonyms of the Ganges shark, expanding the range of the species to Pakistan, Myanmar, Borneo, and Java. The species remains poorly known and is very rare throughout its range.

children.name: Glyphis garricki
children.description: The northern river shark or New Guinea river shark is a species of requiem shark, in the family Carcharhinidae, found in scattered tidal rivers and associated coastal waters in northern Australia and in Papua New Guinea. This species inhabits areas with poor visibility, soft bottoms, and large tides, with immature sharks ranging into fresh and brackish water. It is similar to other river sharks in having a stocky grey body with a high back, tiny eyes, and broad fins.

children.name: Glyphis glyphis
children.description: The speartooth shark is a rare species of river shark, belonging to the family Carcharhinidae. It inhabits coastal marine waters and tidal reaches of large tropical rivers in northern Australia and New Guinea. Despite being a member of the river shark genus, it is also found in near-shore marine waters, favoring highly turbid environments over a wide range of salinities.

children.name: Glyphis hastalis
children.description: Glyphis hastalis is a species of extinct river shark from Great Britain. Modern relatives include the Ganges shark and speartooth shark.

children.name: Glyphis pagoda
children.description: Glyphis pagoda is an extinct river shark from the Miocene.

children.name: Glyphis siamensis
children.description: The Ganges shark is a critically endangered species of requiem shark found in the Ganges River and the Brahmaputra River of India and Bangladesh. Additionally, genetic evidence has shown that both the Borneo river shark and Irrawaddy river shark should be regarded as synonyms of the Ganges shark, expanding the range of the species to Pakistan, Myanmar, Borneo, and Java. The species remains poorly known and is very rare throughout its range.

children.name: Loxodon
children.description: Loxodon — a genus of requiem sharks in the family Carcharhinidae.

children.name: Loxodon macrorhinus
children.description: The sliteye shark is a species of requiem shark, in the family Carcharhinidae, and the only member of its genus. It is found in the tropical waters of the Indo-West Pacific between latitudes 34° N and 30° S, from depths of 7 to 100 metres. It can reach a length of about 95 centimetres (37 in).

children.name: Lamiopsis
children.description: Lamiopsis — a genus of requiem sharks in the family Carcharhinidae.

children.name: Lamiopsis temminckii
children.description: The broadfin shark is a tropical whaler shark, characterized by the broad shape of its pectoral fins. They are classified as requiem sharks of the family Carcharhinidae. This is one of two species in the genus Lamiopsis, the other being the Borneo broadfin shark.

children.name: Lamiopsis tephrodes
children.description: The Borneo broadfin shark is a species of ground shark in the family Carcharhinidae, occurring in inshore waters of the western central and northwestern Pacific Ocean. It is associated with turbid estuarine environments on the continental shelf at depths of less than 50 m and is taken in a variety of coastal fisheries. The species has been assessed as Endangered under criteria A2d on the IUCN Red List due to suspected population reductions driven by intensive fishing pressure across its range.

children.name: Alopiopsis
children.description: Alopiopsis — a genus of requiem sharks in the family Carcharhinidae.

children.name: Alopiopsis plejodon
children.description: Alopiopsis plejodon — a species in the genus Alopiopsis, family Carcharhinidae. Also known as requiem sharks.
